mission.title: Install and Configure Kyverno (Policy Engine)
mission.description: Kyverno is a Kubernetes-native policy engine that allows teams to manage security, compliance, and governance through policy-as-code. It is particularly useful for organizations looking to enforce policies across their Kubernetes clusters without the need for additional languages.

steps.title: Install Kyverno Operator
steps.description:
To install Kyverno, use Helm to deploy the operator in your Kubernetes cluster. First, add the Kyverno Helm repository and update it:
```bash
helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno
helm repo update
```
Then, install Kyverno with the following command:
```bash
helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --version v1.13.0 --namespace kyverno --create-namespace
```
Verify the installation by checking the pods:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno
```
You should see the Kyverno pod running.

steps.title: Configure Kyverno Policies
steps.description:
After installation, you can create policies. For example, create a simple policy to enforce label presence on all pods:
```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-label
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-label
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?
```
Apply the policy:
```bash
kubectl apply -f require-label.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify Kyverno Installation
steps.description:
Ensure that Kyverno is correctly installed and running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno
kubectl get clusterpolicies
kubectl cluster-info
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kyverno Operator
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Kyverno, run the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall kyverno --namespace kyverno
```
Then, delete the namespace if no longer needed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kyverno
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kyverno Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Kyverno, first check the current version:
```bash
helm list -n kyverno
```
Backup existing policies if necessary:
```bash
kubectl get clusterpolicies -o yaml > backup-policies.yaml
```
Then, upgrade to the desired version (e.g., v1.13.0):
```bash
helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --version v1.13.0 --namespace kyverno
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno
```

troubleshooting.title: Kyverno Pod Not Running
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kyverno pod is not running, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n kyverno <kyverno-pod-name>
```
Common issues include insufficient permissions or resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Policy Not Enforcing
troubleshooting.description:
If a policy is not being enforced, ensure it is applied correctly:
```bash
kubectl get clusterpolicies
```
Check the policy for syntax errors or misconfigurations.

troubleshooting.title: Helm Upgrade Fails
troubleshooting.description: If the Helm upgrade fails, check for version compatibility issues. Ensure that the current Kubernetes version is supported by the new Kyverno version.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new policy validation features and improved performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs in favor of newer, more efficient ones.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes versions and enhanced policy management capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated features from previous versions.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Kyverno includes a running operator, applied policies, and verification of policy enforcement through testing. You should see the Kyverno pod running and the policies listed in your cluster.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno
helm repo update
helm install kyverno kyverno/kyverno --version v1.13.0 --namespace kyverno --create-namespace

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-label
spec:
  validationFailureAction: enforce
  rules:
    - name: check-label
      match:
        resources:
          kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Label 'app' is required."
        pattern:
          metadata:
            labels:
              app: ?

kubectl get pods -n kyverno
kubectl get clusterpolicies
kubectl cluster-info

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl and helm installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
